alignment_summary_table.chain_progress: T1 at SEAL only; parallel s13-d1 through P6 IS

authorization: Authorize T1 / parallel S13-D1 alignment review only.

cons: Duplicate effort; parallel chain is already at P6 IS
Substantive parameters identical -> negligible new information
Each phase requires separate fresh operator authorization (P1, P2, P3, P4, P6)

Implementation noise will produce slightly different exact metrics (per s12-d1 dual-chain precedent: 33 vs 48 trades on same data); does not change substantive verdict but adds chain-management overhead
option_A_continue_t1_chain_independently.description: Author T1 P1 plan-lock + P2 phase-2 plan + P3 BUILD + P4 SMOKE + P6 IS in this session's chain
pros: Provides independent corroboration of parallel s13-d1 verdict (deterministic-driver pattern from earlier this session)
Demonstrates byte-stability + reproducibility under different implementation choices
Builds out the operator-shorter-naming T1 chain for parity with parallel's longer naming

cons: T1 SEAL becomes a 'stranded' artifact (sealed but not advanced); less optionality
Loss of independent corroboration opportunity for cost-stress / OOS phases
option_B_defer_to_parallel_s13_d1_as_can.description: Accept parallel s13-d1 chain as the canonical execution chain; do not author T1 P1/P2/P3 etc. T1 SEAL remains on file as alternate-implementation reference but no further T1 phases authored
pros: Parallel chain already has READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST IS verdict; further T1 phases would just reproduce
T1 SEAL remains as alternate-naming reference; non-collision preserved
Operator can authorize parallel's chain advancement without this session's intervention

cons: T1 chain remains at SEAL; no further T1 phases unless verification task arises
Some session capacity unused for direct execution
option_C_independent_review_verification.description: This session continues as review/verification lane only. Author review reports + cross-chain audits when operator authorizes; do NOT execute T1 P1/P2/P3 etc. unless explicit verification need arises
pros: Mirrors established session pattern (S10-D2 OOS reconciliation review; S10-D2 P10.5 sweep; S12-D1 P11 PARK memo all corroborating parallel)
Provides corroborating-verification capacity without duplicate execution
T1 SEAL serves as documented alternate-DA-resolution baseline (different DA-register layout; same substantive choices)
Operator can authorize this session for ad-hoc verification/review tasks per existing pattern

cons: T1 chain explicitly halted vs naturally deferred
No clear advantage over Option B or C
option_D_stop_t1_to_avoid_duplicate_work.description: Explicitly halt T1 chain at SEAL; no further T1 phases under any authorization; T1 SEAL remains as historical sealed artifact
pros: T1 SEAL preserved as historical reference

if_operator_chooses_option_A_continue_in.expected_t1_p6_is_verdict: likely_READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST_with_metrics_near_parallel_159_trades_sharpe_0_1076 (with implementation-noise variance per s12-d1 dual-chain precedent)

next_operator_authorization_natural_opti: Authorize T1/parallel S13-D1 future-phase verification review only (operator-driven; this session does cross-chain audit when parallel advances)

final_recommendation.not_recommended_reason: Option A duplicates parallel effort with low marginal info. Option D loses optionality without clear advantage over B or C.
primary_recommendation_reason: The substantive parameters of T1 and parallel s13-d1 are IDENTICAL (same mechanic, same RSI period, same risk %, same START_CASH, same universe, same data, same windows). Executing T1 P1-P6 would produce near-byte-equivalent results to parallel s13-d1.

Parallel s13-d1 has already reached P6 IS with READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST. The canonical execution chain is established. This session adds the most value as a verification / review / corroboration lane.

The established session pattern (S10-D2 dual chain, S10-D2 OOS reconciliation review, S10-D2 P10.5 cost-stress sweep, S12-D1 P11 PARK memo, S12-D1 P6 IS independent corroboration, S12-D1 lifecycle park report) all support this controller session in a review/verification role complementing parallel execution.

T1 SEAL remains as alternate-DA-resolution baseline; the different DA-register layout (20 items vs 14 items) documents an additional axis of methodology variation that may be useful for future audits.
Option B (defer to parallel as canonical) is the secondary recommendation; A (continue independently) and D (explicitly halt) are not recommended.
secondary_recommendation_reason: Resource-efficient; parallel already has IS verdict
T1 SEAL remains as alternate-naming reference

finding_actual_is_rate_vs_t1_k9_reachabi.implication_for_oos_k9: OOS proportional scaling using parallel's actual IS rate: 34.34/y x 2.0y OOS = 68.7 closed_trades < 100 K9 threshold. OOS K9 is structurally LIKELY TO FIRE at this rate, not just 'borderline-to-clearing'.
finding_actual_is_rate_vs_t1_k9_reachabi.implication_for_t1_seal_k9_reachability_: T1 SEAL's K9-reachability analysis at PLAN/SEAL (46-68/y range; central 57/y) was OVER-OPTIMISTIC by ~30pct relative to parallel's actual 34.34/y on the same audit-clean CSV. Parallel still cleared IS K9 (159 > 100 over 4.6y); my T1 lower-bound estimate of 46/y would predict 92 trades < 100 (would have fired IS K9).
finding_actual_is_rate_vs_t1_k9_reachabi.note: My T1 SEAL's K9-reachability analysis used optimistic literature-based estimates for RSI(2) bi-directional on MNQ.c.0. Parallel's empirical IS rate (34.34/y) is below my lower-bound estimate (46/y). This is informational; it does not modify my T1 SEAL (which is byte-stable). If T1 were to advance through P6 IS, expected outcome aligned with parallel: IS K9 clears (~159 trades); OOS K9 likely fires at proportional scaling. Per DA20=A: OOS K9 sub-threshold disposition is DR1 INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD.
finding_actual_is_rate_vs_t1_k9_reachabi.t1_seal_oos_k9_status_revised_per_parall: LIKELY_FIRES (34.34/y proportionally implies 68.7 OOS trades < 100 K9 threshold)
